coverage_scope: Detailed QBI computation with wage/property limitation

last_audited: true PDF attachment generation for overflow businesses
column12: and richer cooperative/REIT/PTP statement rendering beyond the current structured metadata.

coverage_scope: Thresholds, SSTB phaseout, wage/property limitation, REIT/PTP component

remaining_gap: Form 8995-A resource is still effectively single-business oriented and does not model the full IRS attachment/aggregation surface.

coverage_scope: Part I rental property income/expense matrix plus K-1 page 2 columns

summary: Now implements rental receipts, major expense rows, vacation-home use test, 1099-MISC royalty income, optional royalty expenses, page 2 estate/trust REMIC and farm-rental branches, full Schedule E-style passive K-1 sourcing for Form 8582, prior-year passive-loss carryovers, and special-allowance gating without recursing through AGI calculation.
remaining_gap: Modified AGI in F8582 is still a simplified non-circular approximation, unassigned rental carryover allocation is still coarse, richer K-1 page 2 rental/royalty breakdowns remain under-modeled, and PDF support is still capped to 3 properties and 4 K-1s.

coverage_scope: Flattened rental/royalty facts with passive-loss allowance derivation
summary: Stronger than UsTaxes on derived passive-loss allowance mechanics and explicit rental fact graph calculations; ATS conversion now normalizes missing Schedule E dependencies and aggregates multiple rental properties into a single graph-backed total instead of dropping everything after property one.
remaining_gap: Resource is still a flattened single-column model and does not expose the full Schedule E page 2 K-1 matrix or true paper-form multi-property column behavior.
